channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Governance reforms key to restoring Nairobi's infrastructure
item.description: Nairobi is facing serious urban challenges, with traffic jams, water shortages, polluted rivers, and overflowing waste sites highlighting weaknesses in governance and infrastructure. A new Sh80 billio...

item.title: UK to Convene International Talks on Reopening Strategic Hormuz Strait
item.description: The United Kingdom is set to host a high-level virtual meeting involving about 35 countries to discuss reopening the strategic Strait of Hormuz, which has been effectively closed due to the ongoing Mi...

item.title: Nate Richert, former Sabrina the Teenage Witch actor, reunites with co-stars after two decades away from acting
item.description: Nate Richert, best known for playing Harvey Kinkle on the 1990s television series Sabrina the Teenage Witch, has reappeared in the public eye following a long hiatus from acting. Richert, now 47, had ...

item.title: A Reflection on the Human Cost of War
item.description: This article is a poignant reflection on the human impact of war, focusing on the psychological, social, and moral dimensions rather than the strategic or political aspects. Written as a letter to God...

item.title: Trump Claims Progress in Iran Conflict Amid Declining Public Support
item.description: On Wednesday night, President Donald Trump delivered a 17-minute national address regarding the ongoing conflict with Iran, which has now extended beyond a month. He stated that the U.S. military's co...

item.title: Hoppers Explores Ethical and Scientific Themes in Human-Animal Interactions
item.description: Hoppers, an animated film, presents a story that is deceptively simple yet rich with ethical and scientific questions. The plot centres on Mabel, a young activist who uses advanced technology to trans...

item.title: Achiuwa and DeRozan lead Sacramento Kings to victory over Toronto Raptors
item.description: The Sacramento Kings ended a four-game losing streak with a 123-115 victory over the Toronto Raptors on Wednesday. Precious Achiuwa had a standout performance, recording 28 points and 19 rebounds, his...

item.title: Scheduled Power Outages in Six Kenyan Counties for Maintenance on April 2
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has announced planned electricity interruptions in parts of six counties on Thursday, April 2. The affected areas include Nairobi, Kiambu, Nyeri, Bomet, Bungoma...

item.description: In an effort to ease the financial burden on families amid rising costs of fuel and essential goods, the Marikina City government announced that it will cover all graduation-related expenses for appro...

item.description: In a recent prime-time address from the White House, President Donald Trump claimed that the ongoing US-Israeli military campaign against Iran is nearing its end, while warning of intensified attacks ...

item.title: Police conduct firearms compliance raid at Mkhambathini Municipality amid security concerns
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal police carried out an early-morning raid at Mkhambathini Local Municipality in Camperdown, raising questions over the scale and legality of armed security there. The operation, conducted...

item.description: On April 1, 2026, U.S. President Donald Trump addressed the nation regarding the ongoing conflict in Iran, asserting that American forces had achieved 'overwhelming victories' and that the core strate...

item.title: CCTV Reveals Final Movements of KMTC Student Before Her Death
item.description: In Karatina, Nyeri County, 20-year-old KMTC first-year student Sheila Chebet tragically lost her life, with investigations pointing to her boyfriend, Vincent Bett, as the main suspect. CCTV footage ca...

item.title: Conor D’Monte Receives 15-Year Sentence in B.C. Gang-Related Murder Conspiracy
item.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, Conor D’Monte has been sentenced to 15 years in prison following his conviction for conspiracy to commit murder, a sentence reflecting his involvement in ongoing gang c...

item.title: NASA Reveals Geological Origins of Mount Arkanu in the Sahara
item.description: NASA's Earth Observatory has uncovered the geological origins of Mount Arkanu, a massive formation in Libya's Sahara Desert previously thought to be caused by an extraterrestrial impact. The massif, n...

item.title: Government Directs Investigation into Alleged Fake Graduates at MMUST
item.description: The Kenyan government has directed the Commission for University Education (CUE) to investigate claims that Masinde Muliro University of Science and Technology (MMUST) awarded degrees to individuals w...

item.title: Police Arrest Alleged Drug Dealer and Confiscate R19 Million in Narcotics in KuGompo City
item.description: In a major intelligence-driven operation, police in KuGompo City apprehended a suspected drug lord believed to be orchestrating nationwide drug distribution. The operation resulted in the arrest of tw...
